action_templates.title: Pledge to donate $10 next month
action_templates.description: Commit to making a one-time $10 grassroots donation next month to support organizing and outreach.

action_templates.title: Start a $15 monthly pledge
action_templates.description: Pledge to contribute $15 each month to power sustained campaign efforts.

action_templates.title: Pledge to round up purchases for the campaign
action_templates.description: Commit to rounding up your everyday purchases and donating the change each month.

action_templates.title: Volunteer for Tuesday evening phone banking
action_templates.description: Join a recurring Tuesday evening phone-banking shift to talk with voters about key issues.

action_templates.title: Weekend door knocking in your neighborhood
action_templates.description: Spend a few hours on weekend afternoons going door-to-door with a canvassing partner.

action_templates.title: Remote text banking from home
action_templates.description: Use your phone or laptop to send scripted texts to voters during key evenings.

action_templates.title: Join the online outreach team
action_templates.description: Help monitor social media, answer supporter questions, and share accurate information.

action_templates.title: Share voting guide on social media
action_templates.description: Post our nonpartisan voting guide on your favorite social media platform to help friends make a plan to vote.

action_templates.title: Email a petition to three friends
action_templates.description: Forward a key campaign petition to at least three friends or family members and ask them to sign.

action_templates.title: Share an upcoming event in your group chat
action_templates.description: Send details for an upcoming town hall or rally to your group chat and invite friends to RSVP.

action_templates.title: Attend a local weekend town hall
action_templates.description: Show up in person at a free weekend town hall to ask questions and show support.

action_templates.title: Email your Senator about Climate Bill H.R. 1234
action_templates.description: Use our script to send a personalized message to your U.S. Senator in support of Climate Bill H.R. 1234.

action_templates.title: Join a local immigration justice team
action_templates.description: Connect with organizers in your area who are leading campaigns for immigration justice.

candidates.name: Alicia Martinez
candidates.biography: Alicia Martinez is a former renewable energy engineer and community organizer running for the U.S. Senate to prioritize climate action, reproductive rights, and working families.

candidates.name: David Hsu
candidates.biography: Business owner David Hsu is running for U.S. Senate on a platform focused on economic growth, small business support, and public safety.

candidates.name: Maria Chen
candidates.biography: Maria Chen is a public health advocate and neighborhood association president running for City Council District 5.

candidates.name: Robert King
candidates.biography: Local business owner Robert King is running for City Council District 5 on a platform of small business support and public safety.

candidates.name: Michael Johnson
candidates.biography: Congressman Michael Johnson represents Georgia's 5th District and is seeking re-election on a platform of voting rights and economic justice.

candidates.name: Elena Rodriguez
candidates.biography: Elena Rodriguez is running for Mayor on a bold agenda to expand affordable housing, invest in public transit, and reduce gun violence.

candidates.name: Jordan Lee
candidates.biography: Jordan Lee is a former state senator running for Governor of California with a focus on climate resilience, higher education, and voting rights.

candidates.name: Jamie Walker
candidates.biography: Jamie Walker is a presidential candidate running on a platform of climate justice, democracy reform, and student debt cancellation.

news_articles.title: New Plan Would Cancel Interest on Federal Student Loans
news_articles.summary: Policy experts outline how canceling interest on federal loans could relieve pressure on millions of borrowers.
news_articles.body: A new proposal in Congress would cancel all interest that has accrued on federal student loans, effectively resetting balances to their original principal amounts. Advocates say this would provide immediate relief to millions of borrowers struggling with compounding interest, while still preserving the option for income-based repayment. Opponents question the cost and argue for more targeted relief. The bill also includes provisions for automatic enrollment in income-driven plans and stronger consumer protections.
news_articles.author: Dana Wilson

news_articles.title: How Student Debt Is Shaping the 2026 Midterms
news_articles.summary: Voters with student loans are emerging as a key bloc in competitive districts.
news_articles.body: With the 2026 midterm elections approaching, campaigns are paying close attention to voters burdened by student debt. Polling shows that borrowers are more likely to vote when a candidate proposes clear, specific plans for relief. Organizers are focusing on peer-to-peer outreach on campuses and in online communities to mobilize these voters. Several competitive House races have already seen major ad buys centered on student loan forgiveness and tuition-free community college.
news_articles.author: Samuel Ortiz

news_articles.title: Organizers Win Expansion of Income-Driven Repayment
news_articles.summary: After months of pressure, the administration agrees to expand income-driven repayment and streamline forgiveness.
news_articles.body: Grassroots organizers celebrated a major victory this week as the administration announced an expansion of income-driven repayment programs. The changes will cap payments at a lower percentage of discretionary income and shorten the timeline to forgiveness for low-balance borrowers. Advocacy groups say the win shows the power of coordinated pressure, including petitions, call-in days, and town halls highlighting borrower stories.
news_articles.author: Leah Ahmed

news_articles.title: Student Borrowers Share Their Stories at Capitol Hill Town Hall
news_articles.summary: Borrowers traveled from across the country to speak directly with lawmakers.
news_articles.body: At a packed town hall on Capitol Hill, student loan borrowers described how debt has delayed milestones like buying a home or starting a family. Lawmakers from both parties attended, with several pledging to support new legislation on refinancing and targeted forgiveness. The event was livestreamed to thousands of viewers and generated renewed media attention on the student debt crisis.
news_articles.author: Riley Thompson

news_articles.title: What the Supreme Court Ruling Means for Loan Forgiveness
news_articles.summary: Legal experts unpack the implications of the court's decision on broad student debt cancellation.
news_articles.body: Following the Supreme Court's ruling on the administration's loan forgiveness plan, legal scholars are parsing what options remain. While the decision limited one pathway for broad relief, it left open several narrower tools that advocates are urging the administration to use immediately.
news_articles.author: Priya Desai

news_articles.title: Climate Bill H.R. 1234 Gains Momentum in Senate
news_articles.summary: New co-sponsors and grassroots pressure are pushing Climate Bill H.R. 1234 closer to a floor vote.
news_articles.body: Climate Bill H.R. 1234, which would invest hundreds of billions in clean energy and climate resilience, is gaining new co-sponsors in the Senate. Organizers credit a surge of phone calls, letters, and town halls in key states. The bill includes major funding for environmental justice block grants and union jobs in the clean energy sector.
news_articles.author: Marcus Green

news_articles.title: States Move to Expand Early Voting in 2026
news_articles.summary: Several states have passed new laws expanding early voting hours and locations ahead of the 2026 elections.
news_articles.body: In response to record turnout and long lines in recent elections, lawmakers in multiple states have moved to expand early voting. The reforms include additional weekend hours, more secure drop boxes, and improved language access at polling sites.
news_articles.author: Olivia Martinez

news_articles.title: Travis County Teams Lead New Immigration Justice Campaign
news_articles.summary: Local teams in Travis County, TX, are organizing court support and rapid response networks.
news_articles.body: In Travis County, Texas, immigration justice teams are coordinating court support, rapid response networks, and know-your-rights trainings. The teams have grown rapidly over the past year, with volunteers taking on roles as online organizers, legal observers, and community liaisons.
news_articles.author: Carlos Ramirez

news_articles.title: City Council District 5 Candidates Outline Healthcare Plans
news_articles.summary: Candidates in District 5 debated proposals on clinics, mental health, and prescription costs.
news_articles.body: At a recent forum, City Council District 5 candidates detailed their healthcare agendas. Topics included expanding community health clinics, funding mental health crisis teams, and creating a prescription drug bulk purchasing program. Voters cited healthcare as a top issue heading into the 2024 local elections.
news_articles.author: Nina Patel

news_articles.title: Your 2025 Voting Guide: Key Dates and Races
news_articles.summary: Everything you need to know to make a plan to vote in 2025 local and special elections.
news_articles.body: Our 2025 voting guide compiles registration deadlines, early voting windows, and major races in your area. Share this guide with friends and family so everyone in your network has the information they need to cast a ballot.
news_articles.author: Editorial Team

news_articles.title: Colorado Senate Candidates Spar Over Climate Policy
news_articles.summary: In a Denver debate, Alicia Martinez and David Hsu clashed over fracking, renewables, and public lands.
news_articles.body: During a televised debate in Denver, U.S. Senate candidates Alicia Martinez and David Hsu outlined sharply different visions for Colorado's energy future. Martinez called for a rapid transition to 100% clean energy and an end to new fossil fuel leases on public lands, while Hsu emphasized an 'all-of-the-above' strategy and criticized federal regulations.
news_articles.author: Chris Nguyen

news_articles.title: How to Use the Contact Tool to Reach Your Representatives
news_articles.summary: Step-by-step instructions for sending effective messages to your elected officials using our platform.
news_articles.body: Our contact tool connects you directly with your federal, state, and local representatives. By entering your ZIP code, you can select an issue, personalize a template message, and send it in minutes. This guide walks you through each step and offers tips for making your message stand out.
news_articles.author: Advocacy Team

advocacy_campaigns.title: Support Climate Bill H.R. 1234
advocacy_campaigns.description: Urge your members of Congress to co-sponsor and vote yes on Climate Bill H.R. 1234, a comprehensive package to cut carbon pollution, create clean energy jobs, and invest in environmental justice.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
As your constituent, I am writing to urge you to support Climate Bill H.R. 1234.

This bill would make major investments in clean energy, climate resilience, and environmental justice communities that have borne the brunt of pollution for decades. It will create good union jobs, lower energy bills for families, and help our communities prepare for extreme weather.

I care deeply about this issue because [ADD YOUR PERSONAL STORY HERE]. Please co-sponsor and vote yes on H.R. 1234, and publicly champion its passage.

Thank you for your service.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Pass the Freedom to Vote Act
advocacy_campaigns.description: Call on your U.S. Senators to pass the Freedom to Vote Act, which sets national standards to protect access to the ballot and strengthen our democracy.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
I am asking you to make passage of the Freedom to Vote Act a top priority.

The bill would set strong national standards for early voting, mail voting, voter registration, and protections against partisan gerrymandering. These reforms are essential to ensure that every eligible voter can cast a ballot and have it counted.

Please do everything in your power to pass a strong Freedom to Vote Act without weakening its core protections.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Expand Early Voting in Pennsylvania
advocacy_campaigns.description: Tell your state legislators to support legislation expanding early voting hours and locations across Pennsylvania.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
I am writing to urge you to support HB 4821 to expand early voting in Pennsylvania.

Long lines and limited hours have made it harder for working people, seniors, and people with disabilities to cast their ballots. HB 4821 would add weekend voting hours, increase the number of early voting sites, and improve language access at polling places.

Please vote yes on HB 4821 and publicly support efforts to make voting more accessible for all eligible Pennsylvanians.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Defend DACA and Protect Dreamers
advocacy_campaigns.description: Tell Congress to pass a permanent legislative solution for DACA recipients and Dreamers who call this country home.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
I am calling on you to defend DACA and pass a permanent legislative solution that gives Dreamers a pathway to citizenship.

Hundreds of thousands of young people who grew up in this country are living in limbo. They are our classmates, coworkers, and neighbors. Congress must act to protect them and their families.

Please support and publicly champion legislation that provides a clear, humane path to citizenship for DACA recipients and other Dreamers.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Stop Family Separations in Travis County
advocacy_campaigns.description: Urge local officials in Travis County, TX, to end unnecessary cooperation with ICE that leads to family separations.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
As a resident of Travis County, I am asking you to end local policies that enable family separation and deportation.

Our local agencies should not be voluntarily assisting ICE in ways that go beyond what the law requires. These practices tear families apart, undermine community trust, and make us all less safe.

Please support policies that limit information sharing and cooperation with ICE, invest in legal support for immigrants, and keep families together in our community.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Strengthen Income-Driven Repayment Programs
advocacy_campaigns.description: Tell the administration and Congress to expand and simplify income-driven repayment plans to provide real relief to student loan borrowers.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
I am urging you to strengthen and expand income-driven repayment (IDR) plans for federal student loans.

Current IDR programs are too complex and leave many borrowers with unaffordable payments. Reforms should cap payments at a lower share of income, shorten the path to forgiveness for low-balance borrowers, and automatically enroll struggling borrowers.

Please support administrative and legislative actions that make student loan repayment truly affordable and prevent a new wave of defaults.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Make Community College Tuition-Free in California
advocacy_campaigns.description: Call on state leaders to fund tuition-free community college for California students.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
I am asking you to support SB 210 to make community college tuition-free for California residents.

Rising tuition and fees are pushing higher education out of reach for too many students. Tuition-free community college would open doors to better jobs, help reduce student debt, and strengthen our workforce.

Please vote yes on SB 210 and ensure that the final budget fully funds tuition-free community college across our state.

advocacy_campaigns.title: Fund Community Health Clinics in District 5
advocacy_campaigns.description: Tell your city council member to invest in neighborhood health clinics and mental health services in District 5.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
As a resident of District 5, I am asking you to prioritize funding for community health clinics and mental health services in our neighborhoods.

Too many residents lack access to affordable primary care, dental care, and mental health support. Investing in neighborhood-based clinics and mobile crisis teams will keep families healthier and reduce costly emergency room visits.

Please fight for a city budget that fully funds these services in District 5.

advocacy_campaigns.title: End Cash Bail for Low-Level Offenses
advocacy_campaigns.description: Urge state lawmakers to end cash bail for most low-level offenses and invest in pretrial services instead.
advocacy_campaigns.script_template:
I am writing to urge you to support SB 88 to end cash bail for most low-level offenses.

People should not sit in jail for weeks or months simply because they cannot afford bail, while wealthier defendants go free. Research shows that money bail is not necessary to ensure that most people return to court.

Please vote yes on SB 88 and invest in effective, evidence-based pretrial services instead.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Maria Chen’s healthcare plan focuses on expanding neighborhood clinics, mental health services, and prescription affordability in District 5.
bullet_points: Open two new community health clinics in underserved District 5 neighborhoods within the next four years.
Create a 24/7 mobile mental health crisis response team to reduce police involvement in health emergencies.
Establish a city-funded prescription discount program that negotiates lower drug prices with major pharmacies.
Expand language access and interpretation services at all city-funded health facilities.
Partner with local hospitals to create a preventive care outreach program targeting chronic diseases like diabetes and asthma.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Robert King emphasizes public-private partnerships and efficiency improvements in the local healthcare system.
bullet_points: Offer tax incentives for private urgent-care clinics that open in District 5.
Audit city health programs to eliminate waste and redirect savings to frontline services.
Launch a volunteer-based health education campaign in partnership with local nonprofits.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Maria Chen supports community-based violence prevention and unarmed crisis response teams.
bullet_points: Fund community violence interruption programs led by trusted neighborhood organizations.
Scale up unarmed crisis response teams for mental health and substance use calls.
Invest in youth jobs and after-school programs as proven violence prevention strategies.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Robert King focuses on adding patrols in commercial corridors and expanding security cameras.
bullet_points: Hire additional officers to patrol small business corridors during peak hours.
Expand the network of city-owned security cameras in high-crime areas.
Create a business-led neighborhood watch grant program.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Alicia Martinez backs aggressive emissions cuts, public lands protection, and just transition programs for workers.
bullet_points: Set a national target of 100% clean electricity by 2035 with strong labor standards.
End new fossil fuel leasing on public lands and waters while investing in reclamation jobs.
Create a federal Just Transition Fund to support workers and communities currently dependent on fossil fuel industries.
Increase funding for wildfire prevention, drought resilience, and climate-smart agriculture in Western states.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Martinez supports a pathway to citizenship, humane border policy, and protections for Dreamers.
bullet_points: Create an earned pathway to citizenship for long-term undocumented residents without unnecessary barriers.
Pass permanent protections and citizenship for DACA recipients and other Dreamers.
Invest in community-based alternatives to detention and end for-profit immigration detention centers.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: David Hsu promotes an 'all-of-the-above' energy strategy with a focus on innovation and permitting reform.
bullet_points: Streamline federal permitting for both fossil fuel and renewable energy projects.
Increase funding for carbon capture and advanced nuclear research.
Offer tax incentives for voluntary methane reduction and efficiency upgrades in the oil and gas sector.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Hsu’s immigration platform focuses on border enforcement and temporary work visas.
bullet_points: Increase funding for border technology and personnel.
Expand temporary work visa programs for sectors facing labor shortages.
Require nationwide implementation of electronic employment verification (E-Verify).

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Jordan Lee wants to reduce student debt through tuition-free community college and expanded grants.
bullet_points: Make community college tuition-free for California residents within four years.
Increase state-funded need-based grants for low- and middle-income students at public universities.
Create a state-level loan forgiveness program for graduates who work in high-need public service fields.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Jamie Walker supports broad federal student debt cancellation and long-term reforms.
bullet_points: Cancel at least $50,000 in federal student loan debt per borrower.
Cap undergraduate tuition at public colleges and universities and significantly expand Pell Grants.
End interest capitalization on federal student loans and simplify income-driven repayment.

candidate_issue_positions.summary: Rep. Michael Johnson is a vocal supporter of national voting rights protections.
bullet_points: Co-sponsor and vote for comprehensive federal voting rights legislation including the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act.
Support automatic and same-day voter registration nationwide.
Back protections for vote-by-mail and secure ballot drop boxes in every community.

petitions.title: Protect the Freedom to Vote Nationwide
petitions.summary: Tell Congress to pass strong federal voting rights protections so every eligible voter can cast a ballot and have it counted.
petitions.full_text:
Across the country, politicians are passing laws that make it harder for people to vote, especially Black, Brown, young, disabled, and low-income voters. We need strong national standards that protect the freedom to vote in every state.

We call on Congress to pass comprehensive voting rights legislation that:
- Expands early voting and vote-by-mail options in all states.
- Protects secure ballot drop boxes and accessible polling places.
- Restores and strengthens protections against racial discrimination in voting.
- Ends extreme partisan gerrymandering.

Our democracy works best when all of us can participate. Congress must act now to protect the freedom to vote for everyone, in every community.

petitions.title: Restore the Voting Rights Act
petitions.summary: Urge Congress to restore and strengthen the Voting Rights Act to stop discriminatory voting laws.
petitions.full_text:
The Voting Rights Act of 1965 was one of the most effective civil rights laws in our nation’s history. Supreme Court decisions in recent years have weakened key protections, allowing states to pass discriminatory voting laws that target communities of color.

We demand that Congress restore and strengthen the Voting Rights Act by:
- Re-establishing a strong preclearance system for jurisdictions with a history of discrimination.
- Creating a modern formula that responds to current conditions.
- Ensuring robust enforcement tools so communities can challenge discriminatory laws.

Protecting the right to vote is fundamental to our democracy. Congress must act now to restore the full power of the Voting Rights Act.

petitions.title: Expand Early Voting Access in California
petitions.summary: Call on California lawmakers to expand early voting sites and hours so every voter can cast a ballot that works with their schedule.
petitions.full_text:
Even in a pro-voter state like California, many people still face long lines, limited locations, and confusing rules when they try to vote early.

We urge the California State Legislature and the Governor to:
- Increase the number of early voting locations in underserved communities.
- Guarantee at least two full weekends of early voting statewide.
- Provide clear, multilingual outreach about early voting options.

California should lead the nation in making voting accessible to all. Expand early voting access so every eligible voter can participate.

petitions.title: Protect Drop Boxes in Los Angeles County
petitions.summary: Tell Los Angeles County officials to maintain and expand secure ballot drop boxes in every neighborhood.
petitions.full_text:
Secure ballot drop boxes are a critical tool for voters who work long hours, lack reliable transportation, or prefer to return mail ballots in person. Efforts to remove or reduce drop boxes would disproportionately harm communities of color, seniors, and disabled voters.

We call on Los Angeles County officials to:
- Maintain all existing ballot drop boxes.
- Add new drop boxes in underserved neighborhoods.
- Ensure 24/7 security cameras and clear signage for every drop box location.

Los Angeles County should make it easier—not harder—for voters to safely return their ballots.
petitions.target_name: Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors

petitions.title: Stop Unnecessary ICE Cooperation in Travis County
petitions.summary: Tell Travis County officials to end voluntary collaboration with ICE that leads to family separation.
petitions.full_text:
Families in Travis County are living in fear that a routine traffic stop or court date could lead to detention or deportation because of local cooperation with ICE.

We urge county leaders to:
- End all voluntary ICE detainers and information-sharing beyond what federal law requires.
- Invest in legal defense funds and know-your-rights education for immigrant communities.
- Establish clear policies that keep local services, schools, and courthouses safe from immigration enforcement.

Travis County should be a place where families can live without constant fear of separation.
petitions.target_name: Travis County Commissioners Court and Sheriff

petitions.title: Pass Climate Bill H.R. 1234 Now
petitions.summary: Demand that Congress pass Climate Bill H.R. 1234 to invest in clean energy, resilience, and environmental justice.
petitions.full_text:
Communities across the country are already experiencing the impacts of the climate crisis—wildfires, floods, extreme heat, and storms. Climate Bill H.R. 1234 is a bold step toward a safer, healthier future.

We call on Congress to:
- Pass H.R. 1234 with strong funding for clean energy and climate resilience.
- Prioritize investments in communities that have borne the brunt of pollution for decades.
- Support high-quality, union jobs in the clean energy economy.

We don’t have time to wait. Congress must pass Climate Bill H.R. 1234 now.

petitions.title: Cancel Interest on Federal Student Loans
petitions.summary: Tell federal policymakers to cancel accrued interest on federal student loans and stop new interest from piling up.
petitions.full_text:
Student loan borrowers have paid back their original balances many times over, yet compounding interest keeps them trapped in debt.

We urge federal policymakers to:
- Cancel all accrued interest on federal student loans.
- Set future interest rates at 0% for low- and middle-income borrowers.
- Automatically enroll struggling borrowers in affordable, income-driven repayment plans.

Canceling interest is a concrete step that would provide immediate relief to millions of people and help close the racial wealth gap.
petitions.target_name: U.S. Department of Education and Congress

petitions.title: Fund Community Health Clinics in City Council District 5
petitions.summary: Tell city leaders to invest in neighborhood health clinics and mental health services in District 5.
petitions.full_text:
Residents of City Council District 5 face long wait times, overcrowded emergency rooms, and a shortage of affordable primary care and mental health services.

We call on the Mayor and City Council to:
- Fund at least two new community health clinics in underserved neighborhoods in District 5.
- Create a 24/7 mobile mental health crisis response team.
- Expand language access and interpretation services at all city-funded health facilities.

Everyone in District 5 deserves timely, affordable, culturally competent care.

local_teams.name: Travis County Immigration Power Alliance
local_teams.description: County-wide immigration justice team organizing court support, rapid response, and digital outreach to stop family separations in Travis County.

local_teams.name: Austin Youth for Immigration Justice
local_teams.description: Student- and youth-led organizing hub focused on DACA, deportation defense, and know-your-rights education.

local_teams.name: Travis County Voting Rights Coalition
local_teams.description: Coalition of local groups running voter registration drives, poll monitoring, and ballot access advocacy.

local_teams.name: Austin Climate Action Circle
local_teams.description: Local team pushing for clean energy, transit improvements, and support for federal climate legislation like H.R. 1234.

local_teams.name: Central Texas Student Debt Relief Squad
local_teams.description: Borrower-led team organizing town halls, story collections, and lobby visits on student debt cancellation and repayment reform.

local_teams.name: LA County Immigration Solidarity Network
local_teams.description: Los Angeles-based network providing accompaniment, policy advocacy, and mutual aid for immigrant families.

local_teams.name: Philly Voters United
local_teams.description: Philadelphia team training nonpartisan poll monitors and advocating for expanded early voting and drop boxes.

local_teams.name: Philadelphia Student Debt Justice
local_teams.description: Borrowers organizing around cancellation, campus-based outreach, and federal student loan reforms.

local_teams.name: Chicago Climate & Jobs Alliance
local_teams.description: South Side team fighting for clean air, green jobs, and strong climate legislation at every level of government.

local_teams.name: Chicago Immigrant Rights Organizing Hub
local_teams.description: Citywide team coordinating legal clinics, know-your-rights workshops, and deportation defense campaigns.

local_teams.name: Colorado Climate Voters - Denver
local_teams.description: Front Range volunteers organizing phone banks and town halls around Colorados U.S. Senate climate agenda.

local_teams.name: San Francisco Democracy Defense Team
local_teams.description: Bay Area organizers focused on voting rights, anti-disinformation, and election protection.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Chicago Weeknight Voter Outreach Phone Bank
volunteer_opportunities.description: Join our South Loop team to call voters about registration deadlines and early voting options. Training and scripts provided; no prior experience required.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Every weekday evening, 6–9 PM CT, with flexible 1–3 hour shifts.

volunteer_opportunities.title: National Remote Phone Bank for Climate Bill H.R. 1234
volunteer_opportunities.description: Make calls from home to voters in key states and ask them to contact their Senators in support of Climate Bill H.R. 1234.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30–9 PM ET over Zoom.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Philly Weekend Door Knocking in District 5
volunteer_opportunities.description: Team up with neighbors to knock doors in City Council District 5, share the local voting guide, and talk about healthcare and voting rights.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Saturdays and Sundays, 12–4 PM ET in rotating District 5 neighborhoods.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Travis County Immigration Court Support Phone Bank
volunteer_opportunities.description: Call community members and volunteers to coordinate court support, rides, and translation for immigration hearings in Travis County.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Mondays and Wednesdays, 3–7 PM CT; join from anywhere with a phone or laptop.

volunteer_opportunities.title: East Austin Door Knocking for Immigrant Rights
volunteer_opportunities.description: Knock doors in East Austin to share know-your-rights resources and invite neighbors to local immigration justice meetings.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Every Saturday, 10 AM–2 PM CT, meeting at a central East Austin location.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Central Texas Student Debt Text Banking
volunteer_opportunities.description: Use our texting platform to reach borrowers about upcoming town halls and new student loan relief options.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Tues–Thurs, 6–8:30 PM CT via browser-based texting tool.

volunteer_opportunities.title: LA Immigration Justice Social Media Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help the LA County Immigration Solidarity Network create and share content, respond to DMs, and correct misinformation online.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Flexible weekday shifts; commit to at least 3 hours per week from home.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Event Staff for Philly Town Halls & Rallies
volunteer_opportunities.description: Serve as a greeter, registration helper, or accessibility liaison at town halls and rallies in Center City.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Shifts vary by event; most are weekday evenings or Saturday afternoons in Center City.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Denver Climate Voter Data Entry Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help keep our voter contact data clean and accurate by entering canvass results and updating contact info.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: 2–3 hour remote shifts on weekday afternoons or evenings; training provided.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Francisco Election Protection Hotline Volunteers
volunteer_opportunities.description: Answer voter questions on a nonpartisan hotline and log any issues with access to the ballot.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Weekend shifts during peak election periods; volunteers sign up for specific 2-hour slots.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Travis Immigration Rapid Response Data Support
volunteer_opportunities.description: Log hotline calls, case updates, and volunteer responses to keep our rapid response system running smoothly.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Weekday 2-hour shifts between 9 AM and 5 PM CT; ideal for detail-oriented volunteers.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Chicago After-Work Neighborhood Canvass
volunteer_opportunities.description: Knock doors around the South Loop and nearby neighborhoods to talk with voters about climate and jobs.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_summary: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 5:30–8 PM CT, meeting near 60616 field office.
